chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: Welcome back aliens. My name is Dhavind Reddy and in this video we'll see how do you swap two numbers in Python.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: So let's say we have two variables a and b. The value for a is 5 and the value for b is 6.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: How do you swap these two values?

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: So normally in different languages we have different way of implementing it.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: But one thing is common in most of the language which is you have to use a third variable to swap them.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: Because you cannot simply say a equal to b and b equal to a.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: It will not work right.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: For example, if I try to print the value of a and b now, we are expecting that it should print 6 and 5 after swapping.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: But the moment you run this code you can see we got 6 and 6.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: That means we lost the value 5. So it is not exactly swapping it.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: Now what's happening here?

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: The thing is the moment you say a equal to b that means the value for b which is 6 goes into a.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: And you lost the value which is 5. We wanted 5 right? We wanted to store 5 somewhere.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: And that's why before assigning the value b to a, what you will do is you will take the value of a and you will put that in a temp variable.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: You can use any variable name, doesn't matter.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: So what you will do is you will take a, you will save that a in a temp variable.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: And at the end you will not assign the value of a to b because already a has 6 now.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: So you will assign the value of temp to b.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: So you are storing the value of a to temp.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: Then you are copying the value from b to a and at the end you are simply saying temp is, I mean b is equal to temp.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: It will swap the value. Let's verify.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: And you can see we got the value which is 6 and 5.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: So value for a was 5, now it is 6.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: The value for b was 6, now it is 5.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: Swapping is done.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: But then there is a problem. You are wasting a variable right?

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: And in most of the languages we have different techniques.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: But in Python we have amazing way of doing this and we will see that at the end of the video.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: But then let's try to simplify it. Can we use it in a better way instead of using a third variable?

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: Normally when you go for the interviews they might ask you this question.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: How will you swap two values without using a third variable?

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: In this case what you will do is you will simply use a formula.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: So instead of using a third variable you can use a formula here which is a is equal to a plus b.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: And you will say b is equal to a minus b and a is equal to a minus b.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: Now it doesn't matter from where we got this formula but it works right?

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: Example if you run this code you can still see we got 6 and 5.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: So to understand how it works, so let's see what is a plus b.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: So a is 5 and b is 6. So a plus b is 11.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: And here at this point the value for a is 11 right? Because we have updated it.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: So the new value for a is 11. The value for b is 6.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: So the new value for b becomes 5. So the value for a is 11. The value for b is 5.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: And now we have one more. So here the value for a is 11. The value for b is 5.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: The new values right? And the value for a becomes 6.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: And you can see we got a swapped value. The value for a is 6. The value for b is 5.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: We have done it. We still have one issue with this.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: The issue is, you know if you go in deep, the amount of bits required for 5 is 3 bits right?

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: So 101 is 5 and 6 is basically 110.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: You require 3 bits for 5 and you require 3 bits for 6.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: But what about 11? 11 needs 4 bits. That means you are still wasting one extra bit.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: I know it's not a big number but then still you are wasting a bit.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: And that's why we have one more way which is XOR.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: So you can simply use a cap which is a XOR symbol.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: XOR works in a different way. So what you can do is you can just go to Google and you can find how XOR works.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: And you will find the answer. The advantage of XOR is it will not waste extra bits.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: Okay so that means this thing. If the initial value is of 3 bits, the output will also be of 3 bits.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: You are not wasting extra memory then and you can still see the output. You got 6 and 5.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: So yes there are different ways of swapping. So we got third variable.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: We can use operators like plus, minus or you can use this cap symbol which is XOR basically.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: We have one more which is amazing about Python and which is if you want to swap two values you can simply say

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: and this is not there in other languages okay. This is very unique to Python.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: So you can simply say a, b is equal to b, a.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: See this thing doesn't work in two different lines but it works in one line.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: How this works? Let me run this code and you can see it works. We got 6 and 5.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: So swapping is done. But how it is working?

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: So let's try to understand how it works. So basically when you say b is equal to a right.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: So normally your right side will solve first. So your system will solve the right part first.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: So it will solve b, a. The value for b is 6. The value for a is 5.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: This goes into stack. So we have a stack. It goes into a stack and then it reverse.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: So we have a concept of rotation. So it will rotate these two values.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: So the value of a comes to b and the value of b comes to a and those thing will be assigned here.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: It is just rotating the value. Or in simple terms you can imagine a gets the value of b and b gets the value of a.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: But behind the scene it works in a different way. It uses a concept of two rotation or rotation two.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: So you can simply go to Google and search for rot2 in Python.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: Right, you will get the idea. So even this works.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: So let me know in the comment section if you have any more questions.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: And so that's it from this video. I hope you enjoyed swapping off two numbers.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Swap 2 Variables in Python
chunks.text: See you in the next video.
